body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` **Service directory:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/` **Triggered by:** commit 96635cf97 (#58496) by @azure-sdk ## What Changed Commit #58496 added the `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` management library to the repository, including generated resource types (`DataBoxJobResource`, `DataBoxJobCollection`), custom model extensions, and a README/CHANGELOG. ## Gaps Found - **Wrong service description:** The README states _"This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Customer Insights resources."_ — this is stale copy-paste from a different package and is factually incorrect for DataBox. - **No DataBox-specific Key Concepts:** The "Key concepts" section only contains a generic link; it does not describe DataBox-specific concepts (job types, data copy configurations, job stages, shipping/transfer workflows). - **No inline code examples:** The "Examples" section only links to a generic samples page with no DataBox-specific code showing how to create, list, or manage DataBox jobs. - **No test-backed README snippets:** There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-annotated test methods; only auto-generated samples exist under `tests/Generated/Samples/`. The README therefore has no `Snippet:` placeholders for `Update-Snippets.ps1` to populate.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### Fix the wrong service description in `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` Edit the file. Replace: ```markdown This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Customer Insights resources. ``` With: ```markdown This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Data Box resources, enabling you to create and monitor Data Box jobs that transfer large amounts of data to and from Azure. ``` #### Add DataBox-specific Key Concepts in `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` Replace the existing "Key concepts" section body with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The following are the main resource types exposed by this library: - **DataBoxJob** – Represents a Data Box order. A job tracks the full lifecycle of a data-transfer request: validation, device shipment, data copy, and upload to Azure storage. - **Job stages** – A `DataBoxJobStage` describes the current phase of a job (e.g., `DeviceOrdered`, `DeviceDelivered`, `DataCopied`, `Completed`). - **DataBoxBasicJobDetails** – Contains configuration details for a job, including the data copy settings, contact information, and shipping address. - **Shipping address** – A `DataBoxShippingAddress` describes where the physical device should be shipped. Key concepts of the Azure Resource Manager client library for .NET can be found [here](https://azure.github.io/azure-sdk/dotnet_introduction.html). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets #### 2a. Create a sample test file Create the file `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxJob.cs` with content: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox; using Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_DataBoxJob : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateDataBoxJob() { #region Snippet:CreateDataBoxJob ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; string jobName = "myDataBoxJob"; AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.WestUS; SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource( SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId)); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); DataBoxJobCollection jobs = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxJobs(); // Build job details (DataBox device, storage account destination) var jobDetails = new DataBoxJobDetails( new DataBoxContactDetails( contactName: "Test User", phone: "1234567890", emailList: new[] { "test@example.com" }), new DataBoxShippingAddress( streetAddress1: "1 Microsoft Way", city: "Redmond", stateOrProvince: "WA", country: "US", postalCode: "98052"), new DataBoxDataDestinationDetails( storageAccountId: new ResourceIdentifier( $"/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/mystorageaccount"))); var jobData = new DataBoxJobData(location, jobDetails); ArmOperation operation = await jobs.CreateOrUpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, jobName, jobData); DataBoxJobResource job = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created job: {job.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ListDataBoxJobs() { #region Snippet:ListDataBoxJobs ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); string subscriptionId = ""; string resourceGroupName = ""; SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource( SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId)); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName); DataBoxJobCollection jobs = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxJobs(); await foreach (DataBoxJobResource job in jobs.GetAllAsync()) { Console.WriteLine($"Job: {job.Data.Name} — Status: {job.Data.Status}"); } #endregion } } } ``` #### 2b. Add snippet placeholders to `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md` In the **Examples** section, replace the existing content with: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a DataBox job ```C# Snippet:CreateDataBoxJob ``` ### List DataBox jobs in a resource group ```C# Snippet:ListDataBoxJobs ``` For more code samples, see [.NET Management Library Code Samples](https://aka.ms/azuresdk-net-mgmt-samples). ``` (Note: each fenced block above should use triple backticks as normal markdown fences in the actual README file.) ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README currently has most required sections but is missing or thin in: | Section | Status | Action | |---|---|---| | Getting started > Install | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change | | Getting started > Authenticate | ✅ Present | No change | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with DataBox-specific content (Step 1 above) | | Examples | ⚠️ Link-only, no code | Add snippet placeholders (Step 2b above) | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change | | Next steps | ✅ Present | No change | | Contributing | ✅ Present | No change | Use `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databox` — injects snippet code from `#region Snippet:` tags into README placeholders; verify with `git diff` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databox` — regenerates public API listing files (no new public types expected, but run to be safe) 5. `dotnet format sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [DataBox] Docs: README has stale copy-paste content and missing service-specific examples
